title: Sakata et al. Fish SedDNA Extraction Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"e0mre","text":"Variations of this standardised protocol have been used by Masayuki K. Sakata and colleagues to successfully extract fish eDNA from modern and historic Japanese lake and river sediments. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74cj0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aeiij","text":"The method has been used to recover fish species composition data from modern surface sediments from a lake (Sakata et al., 2020) and a small, natural river (Sakata et al., 2021). It has also been applied to detect target fish species in lake sediments up to 100 years old (Sakata et al., 2022).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8l0kr","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmgrg","text":"This extraction method represents a consolidation of the methods applied in the following publications:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"23ehk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bth19","text":"Sakata, M. (0.33M)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"553pb","text":"TE buffer (pH 6.7) (Prepared with Tris-HCl buffer: 10 mM, EDTA: 1 6.7)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c11mg","text":"NaAc (3M, pH 5.2), ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"28nbd","text":"Ethanol (99.5%),","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fivm4","text":"G2 enhancer (AMPLIQON, liquid type)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f42bh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b5ddj","text":"Kit: (QIAGEN)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"69l20","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6c2un","text":"Required equipment: ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4oak","text":"Thermostatic bath","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5slo6","text":"Centrifuge (capable of centrifuging 50mL, 15mL, 1.5mL tubes)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4tde","text":"Vortex","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79bra","text":"Vortex adapter (adapter to fix tube)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d8263","text":"Lo-Bind tubes for storage (recommended)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: VORTEX samples to mix INCUBATE samples at 94 °C for 00:50:00
ALLOW samples to cool to Room temperature CENTRIFUGE at 5000 x g for 00:00:30
PLACE 9.0 g of sediment sample into a 50 mL tube ADD 6 mL NaOH ADD 3 mL TE buffer ADD 500 µL G2 enhancer Sediment volume can be adjusted but reagent volumes should remain the same
TRANSFER 7.5 mL of supernatant to a new 50 mL tube NEUTRALIZE by adding 7.5 mL Tris-HCL (1M)
ADD 1.5 mL sodium acetate VORTEX well to mix
INCUBATE at -20 °C for at least 01:00:00 Incubation at -20 °C Overnight is recommended
WIPE off condensation from around the 50 mL tube and centrifuge at 5,350xG for 20 min CENTRIFUGE at 5350 x g for 00:20:00

DISCARD supernatant and leave the tube mouth down for a few minutes TRANSFER the precipitate to a PowerBead Tube (Qiagen Kit) DISSOLVE the remaining precipitate with 100 µL of DW then transfer to the PowerBead Tube
ADD 60 µL of Solution C1 VORTEX at max speed for 00:10:00 CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:00:30 Vortex for 15-20 minutes if you have more than 12 samples
TRANSFER supernatant to a new 2 mL tube Expect ~700ul of supernatant, but the more supernatant transferred, the better
ADD 250 µL of Solution C2 VORTEX briefly to mix INCUBATE at 4 °C for 00:05:00 CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g 00:01:00
TRANSFER 600 µL of supernatant to a new 2 mL tube ADD 200 µL of Solution C3 VORTEX briefly to mix INCUBATE at 4 °C for 00:05:00 CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:01:00
TRANSFER 750 µL of supernatant to a new 2 mL tube ADD 1.2 mL of Solution C4 VORTEX well to mix
TRANSFER 675 µL to a Spin Filter CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:01:00 DISCARD the liquid filtrate
REPEAT the above step until all liquid has passed through the Spin Filter
ADD 500 µL of Solution C5 to the Spin Filter CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:00:30 DISCARD the liquid filtrate
TRANSFER the Spin Filter to a new 1.5 mL tube ADD 100 µL of Solution C6 to the Spin Filter LET stand for 00:01:00 CENTRIFUGE at 10000 x g for 00:00:30
DISCARD the Spin Filter DNA is now ready for downstream applications
ADD 30 mL of ethanol VORTEX well to mix
creator: Mark Louie Lopez
